description: We should have the option to show or hide the question. So add maybe a little drop down next to the question icon up top. It allows us to show or hide the question. Also, when we show a new question, it doesn't need to say question up above. In fact, I don't think we need the two answers either. I think we can simplify that as well.

results.identified_issue: Prior turn used a Menu (eye/eye-slash) that needed two taps. Feedback: make it a plain up/down chevron, one tap does the action, and it should point DOWN while the question is showing (was reading as 'up'). Swapped the Menu for a Button that flips showQuestionHeader directly; icon is chevron.down when shown (tap to collapse) and chevron.up when hidden (tap to reveal). Same @AppStorage persistence. If the human instead wanted the chevron.up/chevron.down combined glyph, that's a one-line icon swap.
results.summary: Replaced the show/hide menu with a single-tap up/down chevron
results.detailed_description: Fixed per your note. The eye-icon dropdown is gone. In its place is a single up/down chevron in the top bar that does the action in one tap — no menu to open and no second press. While the question banner is showing, the chevron points down (tap it to collapse the question away); once hidden, it flips to point up (tap to bring it back). The choice still sticks across launches. I couldn't run the app from this flow to screenshot it, so the on-device look is unverified, but the icon and tap behavior are straightforward.

results.notes: There should just be an up-down chevron. It should be down and we're showing it up. It should just be one tap. It shouldn't be tap to open a menu and then press again to actually hide or show it. It should just be one tap to do the action.

results.identified_issue: Read the request as three changes on the Discover feed: (1) a control to hide the persistent question banner (questionHeader under the toolbar in DiscoverView), (2) drop the redundant 'QUESTION' caption on QuestionIntroCard, (3) drop the answer-count line on the same card. Put the toggle as a Menu (native dropdown) next to the existing Browse-questions button and backed it with @AppStorage so the choice sticks. Kept the answerCount parameter because the card's footer still branches on it (no-answers vs swipe-up hint). If the reporter instead meant hiding the question on the intro card itself, that's a different spot — but 'up top' points at the banner.
results.summary: Added a show/hide toggle for the question banner and stripped the intro card down to just the prompt
results.detailed_description: Three changes on the Discover feed, all from your note. First, there's now a little dropdown right next to the Browse-questions icon in the top bar (an eye icon that flips to eye-slash). Tapping it opens a menu with a 'Show question' toggle that shows or hides the question banner running under the toolbar; the choice is remembered across launches. Second, the question intro card no longer prints the word 'QUESTION' above the prompt. Third, that same card no longer shows the '2 answers' line. The intro card is now just the prompt (with its swipe-up / reading footer). I couldn't run the app from this flow to screenshot it, so the visual is unverified on-device, but the logic is straightforward and the header simply drops out of the layout when hidden.
